model.label: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
model.description: Google DeepMind • Preview Gemini model tuned for high-end multimodal reasoning and long-context analysis.

pick.rationale: UConn enters this Round of 32 matchup with a clear statistical advantage, ranking inside the top 10 in both KenPom and Torvik compared to UCLA's standing in the mid-20s. The Huskies boast an elite, balanced profile featuring a top-15 defense that should be able to contain the Bruins' highly efficient scoring attack. While UCLA features a potent offense of their own, their 49th-ranked defense will likely struggle to slow down UConn's execution. Despite monitoring the health of key contributors like Jaylin Stewart, UConn's significant advantage in adjusted efficiency margin and overall talent depth make them the confident pick to advance to the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze UConn vs UCLA Matchup
reasoningStep.summary: UConn has a significant statistical advantage over UCLA, particularly on the defensive end.
evidence: UConn ranks 9th in both KenPom and Torvik with an adjusted efficiency margin of +29.0, driven by a top-15 defense and top-30 offense.
UCLA ranks 27th in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of +21.64, featuring a strong offense but a 49th-ranked defense.
UConn's Silas Demary Jr. is reportedly in good shape, mitigating some injury concerns.

result.title: UConn's Dan Hurley gives injury updates before NCAA Tournament
result.snippet: STORRS, CONNECTICUT - NOVEMBER 19: Silas Demary Jr. #2 and Jaylin Stewart #3 of the Connecticut Huskies dive for the loose ball against Brayden Burries #5 of the Arizona Wildcats during the first half of an NCAA men's basketball game.

result.title: UConn basketball's Dan Hurley gives positive injury updates ... - MSN
result.snippet: Hurley's updates, particularly on Demary , should boost UConn's confidence. Between Demary and Stewart , the former seems to mean more to the Huskies' play on the court.

result.title: UConn Basketball Faces Injury Concerns Ahead of NCAA Tournament
result.snippet: UConn basketball is heading into the 2026 NCAA Tournament with some injury concerns. Head coach Dan Hurley shared updates on the status of two key players - forward Jaylin Stewart and guard Silas Demary Jr. Stewart's status for the first round is uncertain, while Demary is reported to be 'in pretty good shape'.

Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.

The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
